message:
fix(fcp-intercom): strict allow-list for path-segment ids (resolves br-low9w)

Resolves flywheel_connectors-low9w — [REVIEW-A9][P2] connector
security: contact_id / conversation_id were sanitized with a
denylist that missed query / fragment / percent-encoded reserved
delimiters, so adversarial ids could alter the API path.

**Previous behavior (path injection):**

`IntercomClient::sanitize_path_segment` (client.rs:198 pre-fix)
rejected `/`, `\`, `..`, `%2f`, `%5c` but ALLOWED:

- `?` and `#` (so `contact_id = "id?admin=true"` interpolated into
  `/contacts/id?admin=true` — the DELETE call gains a query
  parameter the caller did not authorize).
- `&` and `;` (parameter / semicolon delimiters).
- `%3f`, `%23`, `%26`, etc. — percent-encoded reserved delimiters
  the proxy / Intercom may decode before routing.
- Single `.` (the check only caught `..` substrings — `foo.bar`
  passed). Server-side path normalization could surprise.
- Non-ASCII bytes — Unicode normalization on the server side
  could produce surprising path components.

`delete_contact` formats `/contacts/{contact_id}` and
`reply_to_conversation` formats
`/conversations/{conversation_id}/reply` — both use the
weak sanitizer.

**Fix (mirrors the zapier validate_action_id fix in br-62aa3):**

Switched `sanitize_path_segment` from a denylist to a strict
allow-list:

- Non-empty + ≤ 256 bytes (`MAX_PATH_SEGMENT_LEN` — well above the
  24-char MongoDB ObjectIds Intercom uses internally and comfortably
  above any legitimate `external_id`).
- Per-byte allow-set: ASCII alphanumeric + `-` + `_` only.

Implicit rejection of `/`, `\`, `?`, `#`, `&`, `;`, `%`, `.`,
whitespace, null, control characters, and ANY non-ASCII byte.
Same defense pattern as br-62aa3 (zapier action_id).

**Regression tests (5 new + 1 augmented):**

- `sanitize_path_segment_accepts_valid` (augmented): adds the
  MongoDB ObjectId shape (`5f0c8b1234567890abcdef12`) and
  underscored ids that customers use as `external_id`.
- `sanitize_path_segment_rejects_query_and_fragment`: `?`, `#`,
  `&`, `;` all refused (the bead's named bypass shape).
- `sanitize_path_segment_rejects_percent_encoded_delimiters`:
  `%3f`/`%23`/`%26`/`%2F` all refused — closes the
  proxy-decode-then-route attack.
- `sanitize_path_segment_rejects_any_dot`: `.`, `..`, `foo.bar`,
  `user.42` all refused (the original check only caught the
  `..` substring).
- `sanitize_path_segment_rejects_non_ascii`: `café` and
  zero-width space refused — Unicode-normalization defense.
- `sanitize_path_segment_rejects_oversized_payload`: bounded by
  MAX_PATH_SEGMENT_LEN even for all-alphanumeric input.

The pre-existing `sanitize_path_segment_rejects_traversal` test
continues to pass (the strict allow-list is a superset of the
original denylist — every previously-rejected shape is still
rejected).

241 fcp-intercom tests passing (212 lib + 29 integration; +5 new
lib tests vs. pre-fix). Same connector as br-gs71m (4871a96b
endpoint policy) and br-5g8rj (4ff52943 contacts.delete capability
split).
